summary: Directory-split item subsystem matching PR for src/melee/it, separated from #2617. The final loaded diff makes small code-shape and declaration/lifetime changes across seven item files, with decomp-dev reporting 9 unmatched-item improvements and reviewer feedback against replacing an inline float constant with a data variable for ordering.

key_files.role: Declaration order adjustment in it_802706D0; decomp-dev reported a small score improvement for this function.
evidence_refs: changed_files_jsonl: src/melee/it/itcoll.c added 3 deleted 3 hunks 1
raw_diff: it_802706D0 declaration order changes for arg_item, hit_index, hit
text_corpus: decomp-dev report lists it_802706D0 +1 byte, 96.13% to 96.21%

key_files.role: Introduced z_abs temporary in it_802E72E0 to express absolute-value/sign handling with a different code shape.
evidence_refs: changed_files_jsonl: src/melee/it/items/itarwinglaser.c added 4 deleted 2 hunks 1
raw_diff: it_802E72E0 adds f32 z_abs and assigns z = -z_abs
text_corpus: decomp-dev report lists it_802E72E0 score 98.26% to 98.28%

key_files.role: Changed inline helper itKyasarin_FlipAndFall to accept already-recovered gobj, jobj, and item values from callsites.
evidence_refs: changed_files_jsonl: src/melee/it/items/itkyasarin.c added 4 deleted 5 hunks 2
raw_diff: itKyasarin_FlipAndFall signature gains HSD_JObj* jobj and Item* ip; callsites pass GET_JOBJ(gobj), GET_ITEM(gobj)
text_corpus: decomp-dev report lists it_802EDDC0 +1 byte, 99.15% to 99.32%

key_files.role: Replaced direct hsd_obj access with GET_JOBJ in it_802A7384.
evidence_refs: changed_files_jsonl: src/melee/it/items/itlinkhookshot.c added 2 deleted 2 hunks 2
raw_diff: jobj = link->gobj->hsd_obj replaced by jobj = GET_JOBJ(link->gobj)
text_corpus: decomp-dev report lists it_802A7384 +2 bytes, 99.43% to 99.69%

key_files.role: Adjusted hitbox data access, expanded grapple animation traversal inline, and moved local initialization order in grapple functions.
evidence_refs: changed_files_jsonl: src/melee/it/items/itsamusgrapple.c added 21 deleted 11 hunks 4
raw_diff: it_802B7160 introduces struct spawn_hitbox_4* hitbox_data4 for create_hitbox_4 fields
raw_diff: fn_802B7E34 replaces samus_grapple_anim(gobj) with HSD_JObjAnimAll on item jobj and linked item chain
raw_diff: fn_802B8384 delays fp and link initialization until after PAD_STACK
text_corpus: decomp-dev report lists it_802B7160 +9 bytes, 98.73% to 99.93%, and fn_802B8384 99.55% to 99.66%

key_files.role: Changed declaration order and expression shape in chain motion code; reviewer flagged an earlier constant-to-variable replacement as a regression.
evidence_refs: changed_files_jsonl: src/melee/it/items/itseakchain.c added 25 deleted 32 hunks 4
raw_diff: it_802BAF2C moves attrs declaration after int i
raw_diff: it_802BC080 removes stick_x/stick_y and vy_bias temporaries, using direct field accesses in ABS/sign and velocity limit expressions
text_corpus: decomp-dev report lists it_802BC080 +18 bytes, 98.37% to 99.17%, and it_802BAF2C +1 byte, 99.40% to 99.48%
review_comments: PsiLupan says not to replace constant 1.0f with it_804DD0E0 for ordering

key_files.role: Introduced ip2 alias and earlier attr initialization in shield-hit bounce logic.
evidence_refs: changed_files_jsonl: src/melee/it/items/itseakneedlethrown.c added 7 deleted 6 hunks 1
raw_diff: it_2725_Logic109_HitShield adds Item* ip2 = ip and uses ip2 for velocity fields and it_802762BC
text_corpus: decomp-dev report lists it_2725_Logic109_HitShield +6 bytes, 98.13% to 99.63%

what_changed: The PR was scoped to src/melee/it as a directory/subsystem split from #2617.
Seven item-related source files changed, totaling 66 added and 61 deleted lines in the metadata.
Several edits are local declaration-order or local-lifetime changes, including reordered declarations around PAD_STACK and delayed initialization.
Several edits reshape expressions without introducing broad new abstractions, such as z_abs in arwing laser and direct attribute field use in seak chain velocity limits.
The loaded decomp-dev report recorded 9 improvements in unmatched items for functions in the changed files.
A reviewer flagged replacing a literal constant 1.0f with it_804DD0E0 for ordering as a regression; that exact hunk is not present in the loaded final raw diff excerpt.

smart_moves.text: Kept the PR slice narrow to src/melee/it, making a larger #2617 matching-improvement set easier to review by subsystem.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: Split from #2617 by directory/subsystem so the matching improvements are easier to review
human_pr_text: Scope src/melee/it

smart_moves.text: Used small local code-shape changes instead of broad rewrites: declaration order, temporary aliases, helper argument passing, and expression reshaping.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: itcoll declaration reorder
raw_diff: itkyasarin helper signature change
raw_diff: itsamusgrapple hitbox_data4 temporary
raw_diff: itseakneedlethrown ip2 alias

smart_moves.text: Preserved reviewable per-function progress reporting through decomp-dev output rather than relying only on the preliminary unsplit branch report.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: Per-PR CI/decomp-dev reporting on this draft is the authoritative slice score once it completes
text_corpus: decomp-dev report lists 9 improvements in unmatched items

decomp_lessons.lesson: For matching slices, declaration order and exact timing of local initialization can affect scores even when behavior is unchanged.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: it_802706D0 reorders Item*, u32, and HitCapsule declarations
raw_diff: it_802BAF2C moves attrs declaration after int i
raw_diff: fn_802B8384 changes link/fp from initialized declarations to later assignments
text_corpus: decomp-dev report lists improvements for these functions

decomp_lessons.lesson: A narrow pointer alias to a nested struct can improve matching and readability when many adjacent fields are accessed.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: it_802B7160 adds struct spawn_hitbox_4* hitbox_data4 and uses it for base_knockback, element, shield_damage, hit_sfx fields, hit_aerial, hit_grounded
text_corpus: decomp-dev report lists it_802B7160 +9 bytes, 98.73% to 99.93%

decomp_lessons.lesson: Do not replace an ordinary inline constant with a variable solely for ordering; reviewer feedback classified that as a regression to defer or avoid.
evidence_refs: review_comments: Regression - don't replace the constant with a variable for ordering. We can address this later.
review_comments hunk: it_802A24D0(link, 1.0f) changed to it_802A24D0(link, it_804DD0E0)

naming_conventions.name: z_abs
naming_conventions.observation: Used as a local absolute-value helper name for z-sign handling in it_802E72E0.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: itarwinglaser.c adds f32 z_abs

naming_conventions.name: hitbox_data4
naming_conventions.observation: Names the create_hitbox_4 nested data pointer before copying related hitbox fields.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: itsamusgrapple.c adds struct spawn_hitbox_4* hitbox_data4

naming_conventions.name: ip2
naming_conventions.observation: Introduced as an Item* alias of ip for code-shape reasons in it_2725_Logic109_HitShield; the name is not semantically rich.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: itseakneedlethrown.c adds Item* ip2 = ip

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Declaration-order steering around PAD_STACK and local initialization.
examples: it_802706D0 reorders arg_item, hit_index, and hit declarations
fn_802B8384 moves fp and link initialization after PAD_STACK
it_802BAF2C moves attrs after int i
evidence_refs: raw_diff: itcoll.c it_802706D0
raw_diff: itsamusgrapple.c fn_802B8384
raw_diff: itseakchain.c it_802BAF2C

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Introduce or remove local temporaries to alter expression codegen.
examples: itseakchain removes stick_x, stick_y, vy_bias, and vy_limit temporaries in favor of direct field expressions
evidence_refs: raw_diff: itarwinglaser.c it_802E72E0
raw_diff: itseakchain.c it_802BC080
raw_diff: itseakneedlethrown.c it_2725_Logic109_HitShield

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use access macros where the diff shows they match the intended access shape.
examples: itkyasarin passes GET_JOBJ(gobj) and GET_ITEM(gobj) into an inline helper
evidence_refs: raw_diff: itlinkhookshot.c it_802A7384
raw_diff: itkyasarin.c itKyasarin_FlipAndFall callsites

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Manually inline or expand a helper when the score requires the loop/body shape, but treat this as a narrow matching tactic rather than global style.
examples: fn_802B7E34 replaces samus_grapple_anim(gobj) with HSD_JObjAnimAll on the main jobj and a while loop over linked item gobj hsd_obj values
evidence_refs: raw_diff: itsamusgrapple.c fn_802B7E34

review_feedback.author: PsiLupan
review_feedback.feedback: Regression - don't replace the constant with a variable for ordering. We can address this later.
review_feedback.diff_context: it_802A24D0(link, 1.0f) was shown changed to it_802A24D0(link, it_804DD0E0)
review_feedback.intake_note: This is strong evidence for a review preference against constant-to-variable replacement for ordering in this PR. The loaded final raw_diff excerpt does not include this exact hunk, so intake does not infer whether it was reverted or otherwise resolved.
evidence_refs: review_comments
text_corpus review_comment

follow_up_queries: PR 2624 constant variable ordering regression it_804DD0E0 1.0f
src/melee/it/items/itseakchain.c it_802BC080 ABS lstick_delta attrs x48 matching

notes: The final loaded raw_diff is complete and changed-file metadata is explicit.
Per-function improvement evidence comes from decomp-dev bot report, but no local build, objdiff, or CI logs are included beyond the PR body's local-check statements.
The PR body's preliminary report describes the full unsplit source branch before the split; intake does not treat those broad numbers as final per-PR validation.
The reviewer hunk about replacing 1.0f with it_804DD0E0 is not visible in the loaded final raw_diff, so intake records the feedback without inferring final resolution.
No additional lookup was needed because the loaded PR evidence and standards answer the classification and lesson questions.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Avoid constant-to-variable replacements done only for ordering
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR #2624, review feedback called replacing it_802A24D0(link, 1.0f) with it_802A24D0(link, it_804DD0E0) a regression when done for ordering, with a note to address it later instead.
evidence_refs: review_comments: PsiLupan discussion_r3374331432

accepted_candidate_records.title: Local declaration and initialization order changes were used as narrow item matching tactics
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2624 used small declaration-order and initialization-timing changes in itcoll, itseakchain, and itsamusgrapple, and decomp-dev reported per-function score improvements for the touched functions.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: it_802706D0 declaration order
raw_diff: it_802BAF2C attrs declaration order
raw_diff: fn_802B8384 delayed fp/link assignments
text_corpus: decomp-dev report

source_update_candidates.title: Use PR #2624 as evidence for keeping ordinary constants inline when data variables are only for ordering
source_update_candidates.text: Reviewer feedback in PR #2624 states: 'Regression - don't replace the constant with a variable for ordering. We can address this later.' The hunk showed 1.0f being replaced with it_804DD0E0 in it_802BAF2C. This supports existing literals/data-ordering guidance rather than a new independent rule.
evidence_refs: review_comments
text_corpus review_comment
source_update_candidates.reason: Possible standards-corpus evidence for an already-present principle: do not create or use named data solely to force ordering in a focused code-matching PR.

rejection_notes: Do not promote the broad preliminary unsplit branch report as the final PR validation result; the PR body says per-PR CI/decomp-dev reporting is authoritative once complete.
Do not infer that GET_JOBJ is always preferred globally from this PR alone; the evidence only shows two replacements in itlinkhookshot and helper call arguments in itkyasarin.
Do not infer semantic meaning for ip2; the diff only supports that it is an alias used for code shape.
Do not claim the reviewer-requested constant regression was resolved; the loaded final raw_diff does not include the hunk, but the intake evidence does not explicitly state resolution.
